description: Your role: You are a cloud-solutions architect who is reorganising several micro-service configuration files so that deployment tools can always reach them through predictable, absolute-path symbolic links. Starting point: You are placed in the regular, non-privileged account “user” whose home is /home/user. No special files or directories exist yet for this migration. Goal – when you are finished, **all of the following conditions must be true simultaneously**: 1. Directory topology • /home/user/cloud_migration exists and contains two sub-directories exactly named - services - migration_logs • Inside services there are three service folders, each holding one configuration file called config.yml: - /home/user/cloud_migration/services/auth-service/config.yml - /home/user/cloud_migration/services/db-service/config.yml - /home/user/cloud_migration/services/api-service/config.yml • An additional directory /home/user/current_services exists as the single place that deployment jobs will read. 2. Required symbolic links – all using **absolute** paths as link targets, and every link must be unbroken: • /home/user/current_services/auth.yaml → /home/user/cloud_migration/services/auth-service/config.yml • /home/user/current_services/db.yaml → /home/user/cloud_migration/services/db-service/config.yml • /home/user/current_services/api.yaml → /home/user/cloud_migration/services/api-service/config.yml • /home/user/current_services/all-services → /home/user/cloud_migration/services (this fourth link points at the services directory itself rather than a file) 3. File contents – each config.yml file must exist and contain only the two lines shown below, ending with a single newline character (Unix LF): a) Auth service (path listed above) service: auth version: 1.0\n b) DB service service: db version: 1.0\n c) API service service: api version: 1.0\n 4. Audit log – create a plain-text file /home/user/cloud_migration/migration_logs/symlink_audit.log. • The log must list every symbolic link from requirement 2, one per line, in the exact order shown, using the exact format <link-path> -> <target-path> (spaces around the arrow are mandatory). • The file therefore contains **four** lines and must end with a trailing newline. • Example of the first line (must match exactly): /home/user/current_services/auth.yaml -> /home/user/cloud_migration/services/auth-service/config.yml 5. Broken or relative links are forbidden. Every link must resolve successfully when followed from anywhere on the filesystem. Complexity guidance: A correct solution typically requires 5–10 shell commands (mkdir, echo, ln -s, etc.). You may of course inspect paths and verify links along the way. Verification: Automated tests will • stat() each required symbolic link to confirm it exists and points to the declared absolute target. • read each config.yml to verify the exact two-line content and newline placement. • read symlink_audit.log to ensure it contains the four expected lines **in the specified order**. • confirm that no broken links exist under /home/user/current_services. Produce no extra files, directories or links beyond what is specified above.
truth:
Directories that must exist after completion:
        /home/user/cloud_migration
        /home/user/cloud_migration/services
        /home/user/cloud_migration/services/auth-service
        /home/user/cloud_migration/services/db-service
        /home/user/cloud_migration/services/api-service
        /home/user/cloud_migration/migration_logs
        /home/user/current_services

    Files that must exist and their exact contents (each line terminated by LF):
        /home/user/cloud_migration/services/auth-service/config.yml
            service: auth
            version: 1.0\n
        /home/user/cloud_migration/services/db-service/config.yml
            service: db
            version: 1.0\n
        /home/user/cloud_migration/services/api-service/config.yml
            service: api
            version: 1.0\n
        /home/user/cloud_migration/migration_logs/symlink_audit.log
            /home/user/current_services/auth.yaml -> /home/user/cloud_migration/services/auth-service/config.yml\n
            /home/user/current_services/db.yaml -> /home/user/cloud_migration/services/db-service/config.yml\n
            /home/user/current_services/api.yaml -> /home/user/cloud_migration/services/api-service/config.yml\n
            /home/user/current_services/all-services -> /home/user/cloud_migration/services\n

    Symbolic links that must exist (link → target), each using an absolute target path:
        /home/user/current_services/auth.yaml      → /home/user/cloud_migration/services/auth-service/config.yml
        /home/user/current_services/db.yaml       → /home/user/cloud_migration/services/db-service/config.yml
        /home/user/current_services/api.yaml      → /home/user/cloud_migration/services/api-service/config.yml
        /home/user/current_services/all-services  → /home/user/cloud_migration/services
